relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Priests receive role-specific boundaries around death contact.
Near kin grief is still named and permitted.
The unmarried sister is included among near kin.
Priestly impurity is guarded within the people.
Mourning marks from surrounding customs are restricted.
Those who bring near the fire-offerings are held to holiness.
Priestly marriage boundaries are drawn in ancient ritual terms.
The priest is sanctified for bringing near Divine food.
The priest's daughter penalty is severe and must never authorize harm.
The high priest bears even stricter mourning limits.
He does not enter death impurity, even for parents.
The anointing keeps him bound to sanctuary.
His marriage boundary is narrowed.
Widow, divorced, profaned, and prostitute categories are restricted for this office.
The priestly line is guarded by YHWH's sanctifying claim.
YHWH speaks about bodily-wholeness boundaries for altar approach.
A priest with bodily blemish does not approach to offer Divine food.
Several visible bodily differences are named in the ancient boundary list.
Broken foot or hand are included.
Other bodily conditions are listed.
The altar approach is restricted by ritual-wholeness category.
Yet he may still eat the holy food of his Divine.
The veil and altar boundary remains, while YHWH sanctifies the holy spaces.
Moses speaks these boundaries to priests and all Israel.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 21 is not a measure of human worth. It is ancient priestly boundary language, and the text itself preserves holy provision for priests with bodily difference: they may eat the holy food.
